messages.message:
Error: Cannot call impure function during render

`Date.now` is an impure function. Calling an impure function can produce unstable results that update unpredictably when the component happens to re-render. (https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#components-and-hooks-must-be-idempotent).

D:\AIEKP\apps\dashboard\src\components\chat\ChatBox.tsx:39:11
  37 |
  38 |     const userMessage: ChatMessage = {
> 39 |       id: Date.now().toString(),
     |           ^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot call impure function
  40 |       role: 'user',
  41 |       content: text.trim(),
  42 |     };

Error: Cannot call impure function during render

`Date.now` is an impure function. Calling an impure function can produce unstable results that update unpredictably when the component happens to re-render. (https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#components-and-hooks-must-be-idempotent).

D:\AIEKP\apps\dashboard\src\components\chat\ChatBox.tsx:48:33
  46 |     setIsLoading(true);
  47 |
> 48 |     const assistantMessageId = (Date.now() + 1).toString();
     |                                 ^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot call impure function
  49 |     setMessages((prev) => [
  50 |       ...prev,
  51 |       { id: assistantMessageId, role: 'assistant', content: '', isStreaming: true },
Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'dispatch' and 'initialNodes'. Either include them or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [selectedNodeId, highlightedNodeIds, searchQuery, setNodes, setEdges, initialNodes, dispatch]

fix.text: [selectedNodeId, highlightedNodeIds, searchQuery, setNodes, setEdges, initialNodes, dispatch]
